title: Phenomics Perturbation Profiling
question: Can morphological profile similarity recover mechanism-of-action (MoA) groupings for compounds?
dataset_target: Recursion RxRx3-core (CRISPR KO + compound perturbations) with OpenPhenom (ViT-MAE) embeddings
dataset_actual_poc: LINCS Cell Painting (cpg0004-lincs), batch 2016_04_01_a549_48hr_batch1, 8 plates SQ00014812–SQ00014819, normalized feature-selected morphological profiles from Broad Cell Painting Gallery on AWS Open Data
substitution_reason: RxRx3-core requires a Hugging Face account; LINCS Cell Painting is on a public S3 bucket and is the canonical compound-MoA Cell Painting benchmark. Same scientific question (recover MoA from morphology via NN retrieval).
status: Runnable POC on LINCS; full RxRx3/OpenPhenom path is a target that needs HF authentication
reproduce_command: pip install pandas numpy scipy scikit-learn matplotlib umap-learn pyarrow && python scripts/poc/run_poc.py

headline_result.interpretation: Cosine NN retrieval on untuned morphological profiles beats random by 3–4× at K=1 and K=5 — real MoA signal is recoverable. Drop at K=10 is expected since most eligible MoAs have only 2–3 members.

limitations: POC uses LINCS feature-selected profiles, not OpenPhenom foundation-model embeddings — the full project's hypothesis (FM > classical features) is not tested by the POC
No batch-effect correction (sphering / TVN / CCA); all 8 plates from the same batch minimizes but does not eliminate plate effects
All doses collapsed to one mean profile per compound; dose-aware aggregation would likely improve recall
Only 46 compounds fall into benchmarkable MoA groups on this subset — random baseline is ~3%, recall numbers are noisy
Multi-label MoA strings split on '|'; first listed term used as primary label (lossy simplification)
portfolio_position: Project 5 of 7 — modality expansion outside the GEA thread (high-content microscopy / TechBio phenomics)
role: MoA-retrieval question framing, biological-coherence review of clusters and pathway enrichments. Implementation AI-assisted.
